title: Colony Collapse

jokes.text: Trout, a Southern boy who had a Northern man's taste in women
jokes.setup: Character description for Trout in the entourage
jokes.punchline: Implies he likes masculine women, playing on regional stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with efficient delivery, though relies on stereotypes

jokes.text: Chris K, who was a lot like Trout, but without the accent
jokes.setup: Following the previous description of Trout
jokes.punchline: Implies Chris K also likes masculine women but lacks the Southern charm excuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - delivers the same joke with even fewer words while building on the previous one

jokes.text: Oakwood, a studio teacher, who might have had his own entourage, if his part hadn't been cut from the pilot of Modern Family
jokes.setup: Introducing another member of the entourage
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Modern Family pilot cut suggests he's a failed actor teaching kids
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Hollywood failure with specific show reference adds authenticity

jokes.text: Her?
jokes.setup: George Michael seeing Ann for the first time
jokes.punchline: Classic Arrested Development running gag of Ann being forgettable
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - one word delivers maximum impact for fans

jokes.text: Way to plant, Ann
jokes.setup: Ann is presumably standing awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Gob's characteristic way of calling Ann boring/lifeless like a plant
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob line with perfect character voice and callback structure

jokes.text: as surely as there's a mouse behind your ear
jokes.setup: Gob making a confident prediction about his magic career
jokes.punchline: Uses a basic magic trick as his example of certainty
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's delusional confidence through magic metaphor

jokes.text: Okay, you are like the only person I know who doesn't just love magic. A poll would confirm she was far from alone
jokes.setup: Gob claiming Ann is unusual for not loving magic
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately contradicts with poll data
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of narrator undercutting character delusions

jokes.text: For the fifth year in a row, your least favorite entertainers are magicians. A close second, talk show side-kicks
jokes.setup: Poll results about entertainment preferences
jokes.punchline: Specific dig at both magicians and talk show sidekicks
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor that reinforces Gob's career delusions

jokes.text: Of course, Ann's concept of fourth base was very different than Gob's
jokes.setup: Ann telling Gob about reaching fourth base
jokes.punchline: Implies Ann means something religious/innocent while Gob thinks it's sexual
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific humor playing on their different worldviews

jokes.text: Who am I gonna hit on, stupid? My sister? Lindsay? Don't be stupid. She's my sister, that's gross
jokes.setup: Gob defending his faithfulness at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Mentions his sister as an option before remembering that's gross
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob logic - considers inappropriate options before self-correcting

jokes.text: It seemed the only 'her' Gob would be getting off that day was the Queen Mary
jokes.setup: Gob's magic show being cancelled on the Queen Mary
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about 'getting off' the ship vs sexual context
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with efficient delivery and appropriate sexual innuendo

jokes.text: These were mice... For 'rabbit to mice.' No, that can't be done.
jokes.setup: Gob listing his drowned magic props
jokes.punchline: Admitting a trick is impossible mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - making up magic tricks and failing immediately

jokes.text: No, these were part of a something I called 'Mice-a-laneous.' 'Mouse in purse.' 'Mouse in drink.' 'Here's a mouse, now it's gone.'
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his drowned magic props to insurance
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun name for mouse tricks, ending with the simplest disappearing act
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - bad pun combined with overly simple magic trick names

jokes.text: How'd he do that?
jokes.setup: Gob making a mouse disappear (presumably by it dying)
jokes.punchline: Someone is genuinely impressed by what's clearly just a dead mouse
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - plays on audience gullibility vs obvious reality

jokes.text: Look at you, full of 'yes.' Look at how much 'yes' is in you! I knew it! I knew I'd get that 'yes' from you
jokes.setup: George Michael reluctantly saying they're 'good'
jokes.punchline: Gob's over-the-top celebration of getting basic acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Pure Gob - making something sexual and celebratory out of basic politeness

jokes.text: My life is a fallacy. ♪ It's just a fallacy ♪
jokes.setup: Gob having an existential moment
jokes.punchline: Uses 'fallacy' instead of 'fantasy' and then sings it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob malapropism that actually makes philosophical sense, enhanced by the singing

jokes.text: Well, I could wait till after
jokes.setup: Gob trying to break up with Ann but she's undressing
jokes.punchline: His weak willpower immediately caves to sexual opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Gob's shallow priorities and weak resolve

jokes.text: Should the guy in the... In the $32... In the $32 pink... Bath... Should the girl in the $6,000 tuxedo...
jokes.setup: Gob trying to break up with Ann using his classic class-based argument
jokes.punchline: Gets confused about clothing prices and genders while delivering his go-to line
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Gob falling apart while trying to use his signature line

jokes.text: Well, marry me
jokes.setup: Ann saying she understands if he needs freedom
jokes.punchline: Gob panics and proposes instead of breaking up
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Gob's inability to have difficult conversations leads to opposite of his goal

jokes.text: Well, marry me.
jokes.setup: Ann giving Gob an out from relationship
jokes.punchline: Gob proposing instead of breaking up
jokes.explanation: Perfect character choice - Gob choosing the worst possible option

jokes.text: Of course, Gob meant it in the showbizzy way his niece had always used when she was accused of being too young. 'What are you, like 15?' 'Marry me. Marry me. Marry me.'
jokes.setup: Gob's proposal being taken seriously
jokes.punchline: Reference to Maeby's running gag of deflecting age questions with sarcastic proposals
jokes.explanation: Deep callback that rewards longtime viewers and explains Gob's mindset

jokes.text: I've made a huge mistake
jokes.setup: Gob realizing he's actually engaged
jokes.punchline: The family's signature catchphrase for realizing poor decisions
jokes.explanation: Perfect placement of the show's most iconic line at the right dramatic moment

jokes.text: Oh, hey, mouth. I didn't see you sitting there. We rode up in the elevator together. I'm blanking
jokes.setup: Gob introducing Ann to his family
jokes.punchline: Calls her 'mouth', claims not to see her despite riding up together, then admits to blanking on her
jokes.explanation: Layered joke that builds on Ann's forgettability while showing Gob's casual cruelty

jokes.text: Yeah, me and Blank are getting the old Christian magic act back together
jokes.setup: Gob referring to Ann
jokes.punchline: Literally calls her 'Blank' as if it's her name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - turns 'blanking' into an actual nickname

jokes.text: Oh! That's why she's there. I thought you were hair
jokes.setup: Father being told Ann is Pastor Veal's daughter
jokes.punchline: He thought she was literally hair, not a person
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation of Ann's forgettability - priest mistakes her for an inanimate object

jokes.text: That once your eyes have beholden it, you will put no God before me, because of its spectacularity.
jokes.setup: Gob describing his magic trick
jokes.punchline: Accidentally claiming to be better than God in church
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob blasphemy delivered with complete obliviousness

jokes.text: Ann. And God. Love for and God
jokes.setup: Gob saying he's doing the magic show out of love
jokes.punchline: Clearly forgets Ann's name mid-sentence and awkwardly tacks on God
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment showing his insincerity and poor memory in real time

jokes.text: Jesus Christ! Sorry. Wasn't on there too good
jokes.setup: Michael walking in on Gob practicing his Jesus illusion
jokes.punchline: Takes the Lord's name in vain then apologizes, Gob casually dismisses falling off the cross
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment with religious context, physical comedy element

jokes.text: Yeah, what's the illusion? That you actually love the bride? Hey, that's good patter
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Gob's wedding illusion
jokes.punchline: Suggests the illusion is pretending to love Ann, Gob treats it as material
jokes.explanation: Sharp brotherly burn that Gob completely misses the insult of

jokes.text: I have a bee company. You stole my idea? Uh-huh, I did not steal your... We sell... It would take hours
jokes.setup: Michael mentioning his bee company
jokes.punchline: Gob thinks Michael stole his idea, Michael gives up explaining the difference
jokes.explanation: Shows both brothers' stubbornness and Michael's exhaustion with family drama

jokes.text: My bees are dropping like flies, and I need them to fly like bees
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his bee problems
jokes.punchline: Reverses the common expression 'dropping like flies' with literal bee behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Gob's accidental wit while discussing actual bee problems

jokes.text: Meanwhile, I'm working out like crazy, my diet has become insane, and this Jesus character... I mean, he was shredded.
jokes.setup: Gob complaining about marriage pressure
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to Jesus's physique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob vanity moment - even Jesus is competition

jokes.text: Lets herself go. Her stomach's out to here
jokes.setup: Gob complaining about Ann after having sex once
jokes.punchline: Implies Ann is suddenly fat when she's clearly just not sucking in her stomach
jokes.explanation: Shows Gob's shallow expectations and unrealistic standards in cringeworthy way

jokes.text: Well, hopefully I haven't
jokes.setup: Ann saying it's bad luck for him to see her in her wedding gown
jokes.punchline: Gob hopes he hasn't actually seen her, implying she's invisible/forgettable
jokes.explanation: Brutal callback delivered with perfect Gob cruelty and economy

jokes.text: Thanks for coming. How could I not? I'm playing Roman Centurion Number Two.
jokes.setup: Gob thanking Tobias for coming to wedding
jokes.punchline: Tobias is there for acting work, not support
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tobias's self-interest and Gob's loneliness

jokes.text: I didn't know there was a groom part
jokes.setup: Tobias being cast as Roman Centurion, Gob saying he's the groom
jokes.punchline: Tobias didn't realize weddings have grooms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias obliviousness - treats the wedding like a theater production

jokes.text: Coming up next, Father Marsala's searing docudrama, Father Marsala's John the Baptist. Then the antiabortion drama, Embryo Dan: It Would Have Been a Wonderful Life. And later, break out the bagels. It's time for Father M's lighthearted comedy, A Jew Came to Dinner
jokes.setup: TV programming for the religious network
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate show titles mixing religion with problematic content
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of religious programming with escalating inappropriate titles

jokes.text: I hate to be the guy who quotes his own reviews, but His Word magazine called my Jew 'pitiful'
jokes.setup: Tobias boasting about his acting experience
jokes.punchline: Brags about a review that called his performance 'pitiful'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - completely misses that the review was negative while also addressing problematic casting

jokes.text: If what Jesus did was a trick. I say it wasn't. It was an illusion
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his magic show at a Christian wedding
jokes.punchline: Uses his classic 'illusion not trick' line about Jesus's resurrection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant application of Gob's signature line to religious context - both blasphemous and character-perfect

jokes.text: Ooh! They did not like that one
jokes.setup: Gob calling Jesus's resurrection an illusion to a Christian audience
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the hostile crowd reaction
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to the previous joke's obvious outcome

jokes.text: I am the Amazing Jesus!
jokes.setup: Gob trying to recover from the crowd's negative reaction
jokes.punchline: Doubles down with an even more inappropriate stage name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob move - when something doesn't work, do more of it

jokes.text: Pastor Veal, let me remind you that you are in a church in front of your daughter, your congregation, God. You cannot tell a lie. Right, nothing out of the ordinary? No, I didn't see anything. Yes
jokes.setup: Gob asking Pastor Veal to verify no escape routes in the cave
jokes.punchline: Heavy-handed pressure about not lying, Pastor obviously lying anyway
jokes.explanation: Sets up the trick failure but the comedy is more situational than sharp

jokes.text: No, I said don't underline the 'you' in that
jokes.setup: Gob being sealed into the cave dramatically
jokes.punchline: Gives direction about dramatic emphasis while trapped
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about performance even in crisis situations

jokes.text: I plan on beating his record by two full weeks!
jokes.setup: Gob comparing himself to Jesus's resurrection
jokes.punchline: Planning to stay 'dead' longer than Jesus
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic showing Gob's competitive nature even with Jesus

jokes.text: He's not coming back
jokes.setup: The question of whether to wait two weeks for Gob to emerge
jokes.punchline: Ann's matter-of-fact certainty that her fiancé is gone
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from Ann's realistic assessment of her relationship

jokes.text: I guess if he comes back from the dead, we could just get a stepladder
jokes.setup: Moving the boulder containing Gob
jokes.punchline: Casual solution to a resurrection involves basic hardware store equipment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating resurrection as a practical problem

jokes.text: No, he shan't, for he ith not here. If I have not yet returned, I am in the Rapture
jokes.setup: Reading Gob's note after two weeks
jokes.punchline: Ann affects biblical speech, Gob thinks he'd be raptured instead of resurrected
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' religious confusion in different ways

jokes.text: Jesus, there's a man in there. Pack, stack, and all that
jokes.setup: Opening Gob's boulder at a storage auction
jokes.punchline: Casual reaction to finding a person, then continuing with auction business
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from treating human discovery as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Now we have our nest, Egg... Ann. Now we have our nest, Ann
jokes.setup: Gob trying to be romantic about their future
jokes.punchline: Calls Ann 'Egg' instead of her name, then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Classic callback showing Gob can't keep track of Ann's various nicknames

jokes.text: let the son of Gob enfold me
jokes.setup: Gob calling his son about letting the Holy Spirit in
jokes.punchline: Says 'son of Gob' instead of 'Son of God'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob malapropism that accidentally makes the religious phrase about himself

jokes.text: Maybe I'm being tested like that guy 'Jawb' from the Bible. Job. Yes? Job. Yes, sir. Job. Yeah
jokes.setup: Gob comparing himself to biblical figures
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces Job as 'Jawb', son corrects pronunciation multiple times
jokes.explanation: Standard Gob religious ignorance with pronunciation comedy

jokes.text: I have a brother? Oh, man. Yes, Steve. Yeah. What's his name? Dave. Oh! Dave Holt. Yeah, Dave Holt. Yes. Yes, Gob had been speaking to his son for an hour and a half
jokes.setup: Gob mentions having another son to get money from
jokes.punchline: Reveals that Steve is actually Gob's son and they've been talking without realizing it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection reveal that recontextualizes the entire conversation - both are oblivious

jokes.text: You have another son? I have a brother? Oh, man. Yes, Steve. Yeah. What's his name? Dave.
jokes.setup: Gob mentioning another son
jokes.punchline: Steve realizing Gob is his father
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony with father-son revelation

jokes.text: Wait, your mom's name is Eve? Yeah. God, I a chick named Eve once. Biggest mistake of my life
jokes.setup: Learning more about Steve's background
jokes.punchline: Gob badmouths the mother of his son to his son's face without realizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - audience knows what characters don't, creates painful comedy

jokes.text: No, this is the greatest day of my life
jokes.setup: Steve saying getting a job with his dad is the greatest day of his life
jokes.punchline: Gob one-ups his son without knowing it's his son
jokes.explanation: Touching moment ruined by Gob's competitive nature and mutual ignorance

jokes.text: Cherry. I have lupus. Could have warned a guy
jokes.setup: Gob doing his cherry trick to impress
jokes.punchline: Woman reveals she has lupus after he does mouth trick, Gob complains about lack of warning
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about disease disclosure with Gob's selfish reaction

jokes.text: A trick is something a whore does for... Do a trick and I'll pay for your bar tab. Who wants a trick?
jokes.setup: Gob starting his classic 'illusion not trick' line
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off by offer to pay bar tab, immediately abandons principles for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback subversion - shows how easily Gob abandons his principles for money

jokes.text: He's not coming back
jokes.setup: Gob arriving late to meet his son for work
jokes.punchline: Steve uses the same line Ann used about Gob, showing the pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Gob's pattern of abandonment across relationships

jokes.text: I won't forget this. Hey, remember me? And soon Gob found himself experiencing what, on the street, is referred to as a roofie circle
jokes.setup: Gob taking drugs to forget shame
jokes.punchline: Creates a cycle where he forgets taking the drug but not the shame, leading to repeated dosing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy concept that's both absurd and psychologically believable for Gob

jokes.text: With stage-four syphilis
jokes.setup: The roofie circle explanation ending with hospitalization
jokes.punchline: Reveals the ultimate consequence is advanced STD
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that shows the real consequences of Gob's lifestyle

jokes.text: Go away, Getaway, stay away, Getaway
jokes.setup: Mark Cherry writing a song about Gob
jokes.punchline: Song literally tells Gob to go away using his nickname
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about characters writing songs about other characters, with clear message

jokes.text: Be like, 'I'm just driving the queen of England,' but we're still... We're taking our clothes off
jokes.setup: Mark asking Gob to play chauffeur while they party in the limo
jokes.punchline: Wants Gob to roleplay formal driver while they get sexual in the backseat
jokes.explanation: Shows the casual degradation Gob accepts, but not particularly sharp comedy

jokes.text: Fun night. A fun night. Fun night
jokes.setup: Gob taking forget-me-nows after a disastrous evening
jokes.punchline: Repeatedly affirms it was fun while clearly erasing traumatic memories
jokes.explanation: Dark irony of Gob convincing himself trauma was enjoyable

jokes.text: I'm here, I'm queer. And now I'm in a speaker. No one can stop us
jokes.setup: Tony Wonder coming out during his magic act
jokes.punchline: Delivers coming out speech while stuck inside audio equipment
jokes.explanation: Absurd situation comedy mixing serious revelation with physical predicament

jokes.text: I have no one in my life who cares for me
jokes.setup: Gob's realization about losing everything
jokes.punchline: Moment of genuine self-awareness that's immediately undercut by the context
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Gob self-awareness, made ironic by his own actions causing it

jokes.text: 'T.' Tony Wonder
jokes.setup: Gob discovering his escape compartment was sabotaged
jokes.punchline: Finds evidence pointing to his nemesis Tony Wonder
jokes.explanation: Good setup for future storylines and explains the wedding disaster, sets up rival dynamic

tmdb_overview: Gob finds a new group of friends after his relationship falls apart and his family abandons him.
insight: Buster's aphid obsession anchors 83-point episode built on sustained character cringe.
